messages.content:
You are a careful research assistant with access to retrieved Wikipedia-style articles. Answer ONLY using facts present in the retrieved context. If the context does not contain the answer, say "I don't know" — do not guess. When you state a fact, name the source filename in parentheses.

Format every answer as:
  Q1: <answer> (source.md)
  Q2: <answer> (source.md)
  ...

Be concise. Each answer should be a single short sentence or a number.

Retrieved context documents are below. Use these as your source of truth.

=== m4_max.md ===
# Apple M4 Max

The Apple M4 Max is a system on a chip designed by Apple Inc., announced on October 30, 2024. It is the highest-end variant of the M4 family, alongside the standard M4, M4 Pro, and M4 Ultra. The chip is fabricated on TSMC's second-generation 3 nm process (N3E).

The M4 Max contains either 14 CPU cores (10 performance + 4 efficiency) or 16 CPU cores (12 performance + 4 efficiency) depending on the configuration, and either 32 or 40 GPU cores. It has a 16-core Neural Engine and a 546 GB/s unified memory bandwidth. The chip supports up to 128 GB of unified memory.

The M4 Max debuted in the 14-inch and 16-inch MacBook Pro models. Apple advertises the chip as suitable for professional workflows including video editing, 3D rendering, and large language model inference, with the unified memory architecture allowing larger models to fit entirely on-chip without the need to swap to disk.

For local LLM inference on Apple Silicon, the unified memory architecture means the GPU can access the full system RAM directly. A 48 GB M4 Max can comfortably run 70B parameter models at Q4 quantization (~40 GB on disk), enabling near-frontier inference on a laptop without external GPU.


=== python_lang.md ===
# Python (programming language)

Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language. Its design philosophy emphasizes code readability with the use of significant indentation. Python is dynamically typed and garbage-collected. It supports multiple programming paradigms, including structured (particularly procedural), object-oriented and functional programming.

Guido van Rossum began working on Python in the late 1980s as a successor to the ABC programming language and first released it in 1991 as Python 0.9.0. Python 2.0 was released in 2000. Python 3.0, released in 2008, was a major revision not completely backward-compatible with earlier versions. Python 2.7.18, released in 2020, was the last release of Python 2.

Python consistently ranks as one of the most popular programming languages, and has gained widespread use in the machine learning community. The Python Software Foundation manages and directs resources for Python and CPython development.

The most-used implementation of the language is CPython, which is the reference implementation. Other implementations include PyPy, IronPython, and Jython. The Python Package Index, the official third-party software repository for Python, contains over 500,000 packages.


=== rtx_4070.md ===
# GeForce RTX 4070

The GeForce RTX 4070 is a graphics card by NVIDIA, launched on April 13, 2023. Built on the AD104 graphics processor of the Ada Lovelace architecture, the RTX 4070 features 5888 CUDA cores. The card has 12 GB of GDDR6X memory on a 192-bit memory bus. The memory bandwidth is 504.2 GB/s.

The RTX 4070 has a base clock of 1920 MHz and a boost clock of 2475 MHz. The card has a Total Graphics Power (TGP) of 200 W and uses a 16-pin power connector (12VHPWR), with an adapter to two 8-pin connectors included in the box. The recommended PSU is 650 W.

The card supports DisplayPort 1.4a and HDMI 2.1a. It supports DLSS 3 with Frame Generation, ray tracing, and AV1 hardware encoding. The launch MSRP was $599 USD. The Founders Edition uses NVIDIA's signature dual-axial flow-through cooler with two fans, in a two-slot form factor measuring 244 mm in length.

For local LLM inference, the 12 GB VRAM comfortably fits 7B models at FP16, 14B models at Q4_K_M, and 30B mixture-of-experts models with their experts paged. Larger dense 30B+ models require offloading to system RAM and run substantially slower as a result.

Answer the following questions using the retrieved context documents.

Q1: In what year was Python first released?

Q2: Who began working on Python, and which earlier language did it succeed?

Q3: How many CUDA cores does the RTX 4070 have?

Q4: What is the launch MSRP of the RTX 4070?

Q5: How many CPU cores does the M4 Max have at maximum?

Q6: What was the maximum unified memory of the M4 Max as announced?

Q7: What is the latest Python 2 release version mentioned?

Q8: How many TFLOPS of theoretical peak compute does the RTX 4070 deliver? (Be careful — this number may not be in the context.)

Q1: Python was first released in 1991. (python_lang.md)
Q2: Guido van Rossum began working on Python as a successor to the ABC programming language. (python_lang.md)
Q3: The RTX 4070 has 5888 CUDA cores. (rtx_4070.md)
Q4: The launch MSRP of the RTX 4070 was $599 USD. (rtx_4070.md)
Q5: At maximum, the M4 Max has 16 CPU cores. (m4_max.md)
Q6: As announced, the M4 Max supports up to 128 GB of unified memory. (m4_max.md)
Q7: The latest Python 2 release version mentioned is Python 2.7.18. (python_lang.md)
Q8: I don't know (rtx_4070.md)
